title: Exposing Docker sockets is security-sensitive
description: Exposing Docker sockets is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity HotspotcweExposing Docker sockets can lead to compromise of the host systems.The Docker daemon provides an API to access its functionality, for example through a UNIX domain socket. Mounting the Docker socket into a container allows the container to control the Docker daemon of the host system, resulting in full access over the whole system. A compromised or rogue container with access to the Docker socket could endanger the integrity of the whole Kubernetes cluster.Ask Yourself WhetherThe Pod is untrusted or might contain vulnerabilities.There is a risk if you answered yes to this question.Recommended Secure Coding PracticesIt is recommended to never add a Docker socket as a volume to a P
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
- name: Create Pod
  kubernetes.core.k8s:
    definition:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
        name: test
      spec:
        containers:
        - image: k8s.gcr.io/test-webserver
          name: test-container
          volumeMounts:
          - mountPath: /var/run/docker.sock
            name: test-volume
        volumes:
        - name: test-volume
          hostPath:
            path: /var/run/docker.sock # Sensitive
            type: Socket
compliant:
- name: Create Pod
  kubernetes.core.k8s:
    definition:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
        name: test
      spec:
        containers:
        - image: k8s.gcr.io/test-webserver
          name: test-container
